mnemonics: WATERMARKS are like a secret DIGITAL SIGNATURE. Think of an artist signing their painting (the data) to prove it's authentic and to track if someone steals it.
keyPoints: Watermarking embeds a hidden, indelible signal into digital data (images, videos, documents) to prove ownership or authenticity.
It is used for copyright protection, traitor tracing, and tamper detection.
A robust watermark must survive common transformations like compression, cropping, and filtering.
commonMistakes: Confusing watermarking with encryption (watermarking doesn't hide content, it adds a mark to it).
Assuming all watermarks are perceptually invisible; some are visible by design.
